name: Stealth Kill
description: A high-reward attack performed from concealment, surprise, or a vulnerable target state.
design_notes: Stealth kill combines AI awareness, positioning, animation alignment, damage rules, and moral or systemic consequences. The mechanic should make eligibility visible enough to feel earned but not so automatic that stealth collapses into prompt hunting.
design_purpose: Reward players for reaching a target without raising alarm.
Provide a decisive alternative to open combat when positioning and awareness conditions are met.
Create tension around body discovery, noise, animation commitment, and nearby witnesses.
player_fantasy: The player feels precise and unseen, removing a threat through planning rather than direct confrontation.

parameters.name: takedown_angle_degrees
parameters.description: Facing or rear-arc requirement for eligibility.

parameters.name: takedown_prompt_distance
parameters.description: Maximum distance for showing the takedown prompt.

parameters.name: execution_lock_frames
parameters.description: Commitment frames where player and target are locked into the action.

parameters.name: takedown_noise_radius
parameters.description: Noise event emitted after the takedown.

parameters.name: witness_alert_radius
parameters.description: Area checked for AI that can see or hear the takedown.

edge_cases: The target turning during prompt display should either preserve eligibility briefly or cancel before input.
Nearby witnesses need line-of-sight and hearing checks before alarm state changes.
Large enemies, bosses, vehicles, and scripted NPCs need explicit immune or special-case metadata.
Co-op players attempting the same target must avoid duplicate takedown ownership.
common_bugs: Prompt appears through walls because eligibility uses distance but not visibility.
The target dies before animation alignment completes, leaving the player locked in place.
Body discovery logic runs before the takedown finishes and alerts the room instantly.
Networked takedown authority is split and both players receive kill credit or conflicting animations.
balancing_notes: Instant takedowns should be gated by awareness, enemy type, animation commitment, or post-kill consequences.
Noise and body management keep stealth kills from removing all encounter pressure.
accessibility_notes: Use a clear prompt with configurable size and avoid color-only eligibility changes.
Allow prompt hold or confirm settings so accidental takedowns do not punish motor control differences.

implementation_notes.unity: Model startup, active, recovery, and cancel windows as data, then drive hitboxes, animation events, and damage application from one combat state source. For Stealth Kill, validate this path with parameters such as takedown_angle_degrees and takedown_prompt_distance.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D hit volumes with frame-window state on the actor; avoid placing damage authority inside animation-only callbacks. For Stealth Kill, validate this path with parameters such as takedown_angle_degrees and takedown_prompt_distance.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement the action as a Gameplay Ability or Actor Component with montage notifies for windows and authoritative damage on the server when networked. For Stealth Kill, validate this path with parameters such as takedown_angle_degrees and takedown_prompt_distance.
implementation_notes.web: Update combat windows in the fixed simulation step, evaluate hurtbox overlap from cached transforms, and emit presentation events separately from damage state. For Stealth Kill, validate this path with parameters such as takedown_angle_degrees and takedown_prompt_distance.
